frameworks.name: perceptual history / serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: past percepts bias current interpretation under ambiguity

frameworks.name: bistable perception
frameworks.one_line: ambiguous stimuli alternate between rival interpretations

frameworks.name: internal vs external mode of sensory processing
frameworks.one_line: slow fluctuation between prior-driven and evidence-driven perception

frameworks.name: computational modeling of perceptual inference
frameworks.one_line: model formalises balance between history and sensory evidence

core_question: How does the balance between perceptual history and current sensory evidence vary over time when stimuli are only partially ambiguous?
key_assumptions: perception integrates prior percepts with current sensory evidence
partial ambiguity is more ecologically relevant than fully ambiguous stimuli
the prior/evidence weighting can vary across time within an observer
manipulations: extended viewing intervals allowing temporal fluctuations to emerge
key_findings: perception was at times dominated by perceptual history, at other times by sensory evidence
these regimes alternated as slow fluctuations spanning up to several minutes
the past/present balance is not stationary
interpretations: conscious perception of ambiguous stimuli is modulated by slow shifts between internally and externally oriented processing modes
perceptual history and sensory evidence are dynamically reweighted rather than fixed
